summary: Patrick McKenzie provides updates on several fronts: his SEO article ranked #2 on Google for a competitive search term within 48 hours, demonstrating the blogging-for-SEO strategy he advocates. He discusses redesigning website icons after trademark guideline concerns, a CrazyEgg/e-junkie compatibility bug affecting his sales funnel, and shares good news about a promising job interview with a Japanese software company. The post illustrates the day-to-day reality of running a micro-ISV while job hunting in Japan.
key_points: A blog post targeting 'increase software sales' reached Google's #2 position in 48 hours, proving the SEO-through-blogging strategy works even for competitive terms with 30 million results
WordPress's URL structure, title tags, and keyword repetition combined with high-pagerank inbound links drove rapid Google ranking gains
He redesigned purchase page icons after being told they violated Microsoft and Apple trademark guidelines, noting the guidelines are broader than what the law actually requires
A JavaScript incompatibility between e-junkie's cart and CrazyEgg was potentially costing him money, showing how tool conflicts in the sales funnel can silently hurt revenue
His job search in Japan was progressing well, with a strong interview at a 250-person company that valued both his Java skills and his side-project web development experience
money_quotes: I predicted I would be on the front page of Google for the string 'increase software sales' within a week. It actually took about 48 hours and I'm number two.
Your niche also has competitive search words which a decently popular blog article could crack fairly quickly.
Its my personal opinion that their guidelines are poorly considered and substantially broader than the rights the law actually gives them, but I'm following them as a professional courtesy.
As the incompatibility was potentially costing me money I disabled CrazyEgg and sent them an email.
They also have dreams of Web 2.0ifying a few of their properties and were extraordinarily pleased to hear that my 'extracurricular interests' had me learning Javascript, Rails, and MVC frameworks.
key_insight: A well-targeted blog post with proper SEO fundamentals can rank on Google's front page for competitive terms within days, demonstrating that content marketing is one of the most accessible growth levers for small software businesses.
